resources.string: Gold dust fills the lungs; the hands still reach for more.
GOLD DUST FILLS THE LUNGS. HANDS STILL REACH FOR MORE.

Coins climb in feverish heaps and throw a wrong light over the vault. Diamonds rain from the ceiling. A white bone hand breaks under bracelets too heavy to love, while a living collector turns to gold from the fingertips inward. He gasps through glittering dust. He owns rooms, crowns, names, and keys, yet the only thought left is the wound of not owning all.
COINS RISE. DIAMONDS FALL. BONE BREAKS UNDER JEWELS. GOLD DUST IS BREATHED. THE VAULT IS FULL. IT IS NOT ENOUGH.
